author: mark
text:
VERDICT — bl-8a9a in-place migration is SAFE for the live bare hub, with preconditions. Verified empirically on faithful throwaway bare-hub copies (legacy .git + .balls/worktree layout, real 1454-commit task history, real plugin config), migrated with a bl built from 87fdd38. No committed data is lost.

(a) Full history — PASS. fetch + reset --hard FETCH_HEAD transfers the whole branch: 1454 commits + 1 seed commit = 1455 in .balls/state-repo; oldest commit identical to source. Not just HEAD.
(b) Durable tracker + sync — PASS. init_repo wires the code repo's origin (GitHub on the live repo) as the state-repo origin. Post-migration bl prime round-trips: pushed the seed commit to the tracker; a separate clone fetched it cleanly.
(c) Old-bl clones / shared origin — PASS. retire_legacy_worktree's 'git branch -D balls/tasks' is purely local; no deletion is pushed. The shared tracker keeps balls/tasks. An independent old-bl 0.4.2 legacy clone pinned to pre-migration state ran bl prime, fast-forwarded through the seed commit, bl list worked. Not stranded.
(d) Plugin absorb — PARTIAL. *.json are correctly moved into .balls/state-repo/.balls/plugins and the symlink is right, but left UNCOMMITTED (seed() commits before absorb_plugins_dir runs; bl sync does not commit them). Filed bl-73bb. The task's 'main-branch deletion drift gets committed' is inaccurate — migration never touches main; .balls/plugins/*.json stay on main as stale duplicates; on a bare hub no working-tree drift surfaces. Reconciled later by bl-e609 (project.json).

EXTRA / riskiest: migration SILENTLY DISCARDS uncommitted state in the legacy .balls/worktree (verified: an injected modified task file and an untracked new file were both gone post-migration). Filed bl-c7b5.

PRECONDITIONS before 'make install' on the live repo:
1. Live .balls/worktree has uncommitted state (D bl-04ed.notes.jsonl) and balls/tasks is 5 commits ahead of origin. The 5 commits are committed -> safe (fetch captures them, still pushable). The uncommitted deletion would be silently dropped — commit/quiesce .balls/worktree with the old bl first.
2. Run the migration when no other agent is mid-task — a concurrent old-bl agent in a worktree breaks post-migration (.balls/worktree gone) and must re-prime with the new bl.
3. After migration, run a bl plugin command (or manually commit+push .balls/state-repo/.balls/plugins/*) so plugin config reaches the tracker.

Migration is recoverable — it destroys nothing not reconstructible from the durable tracker. Safe to proceed once preconditions are met. Follow-ups: bl-73bb, bl-c7b5.
